name: Roast Goose with Potato Pierogies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Remove the neck and giblets; remove any large pieces of fat from inside the goose and neck cavity; reserve the neck and liver.
3. Rinse the goose and pat dry with paper towels.
4. In a small bowl, combine 2 tablespoons thyme and the salt and pepper.
5. Rub the bird with 2 tablespoons oil, then rub with the thyme mixture.
6. Tuck the wings under and tie the legs together.
7. Place the goose, breast-side up, on a rack in a large roasting pan. Pierce the skin all over with a fork.
8. Roast the goose for 1 hour. During roasting, pierce the skin several times with a fork.
9. To keep the fat from smoking, remove it from the pan with a baster or cooking spoon.
10. Reduce the oven temperature to 325°F. Roast for 2 to 2 1/2 hours longer, or until the internal temperature registers 180 to 185°F on a meat thermometer, and the juices run clear when the bird is pierced with a knife.
11. Continue to pierce the skin and remove the fat while roasting.
12. While the goose is roasting, finely chop the neck meat and liver for making the gravy.
13. Refrigerate until ready to use.
14. When the goose is almost done, set a large pot of water to boil. Cook the pierogies according to the package directions.
15. Meanwhile, in a large skillet, sauté the onions in the remaining 2 tablespoons oil until golden; remove from heat.
16. Drain the pierogies well; add the cooked onions.
17. Mix lightly to combine. Cover and keep warm.
18. Place the goose on a large serving platter; cover loosely with foil to keep warm.
19. Remove the remaining fat from the pan and place the pan drippings into a glass measure. Let any fat rise to the surface and discard. Reserve 1/3 cup of the drippings.
20. Set the roasting pan on top of the stove. Add the reserved drippings.
21. Sauté the chopped neck meat and liver for 5 minutes or until cooked.
22. Blend in the flour; cook, stirring constantly, until bubbly.
23. Stir in the broth; cook and stir until the mixture thickens and bubbles, about 3 minutes.
24. Stir in the remaining teaspoon of thyme.
25. Pour into a gravy boat. Arrange the pierogies around the goose or place in a separate bowl. Serve with gravy.
